fields.description:
## Context

Three production Jira-sync bugs escaped our tests this cycle, all the same CLASS ‚Äî a gap at a *seam* that no test or probe spanned:

- **Inbound comments (bug 0ee6):** the inbound differ read snapshot key `comments`, but the fetcher writes the nested Jira-REST key `comment` ‚Äî producer and consumer were unit-tested in isolation, so their shared shape was never jointly exercised.
- **Issue-links both directions (bug 3f04):** outbound link dispatch existed only in a *typed-leaf* applier that production does not use (production routes through the *batch* applier, which dropped links); inbound links were invisible because the snapshot never carried `issuelinks`.
- **A live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED** (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap ‚Ä¶ NOT-SYNCED`), so the defect could never be detected.

Root causes: (1) no producer‚Üîconsumer contract ‚Äî differs are unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits; (2) two outbound applier paths drift (tests cover the dead typed leaf; production uses the batch path; capabilities were re-added one bug at a time ‚Äî parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04); (3) sub-op success is silent (the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None`, never `links_applied`/`comments_applied`); (4) live probes are manual, not in CI, do not round-trip relationships, and can encode bugs as expected.

Deep research (Pact consumer-driven contracts; vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation; Google SWE-book "verified fakes" and its change-detector warning; and how real Jira clients test ‚Äî `jira-python` uses a live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox, while `jira-cli` serves on-disk fixtures *captured from real Jira* THROUGH its production serialization via an in-process mock) converges on a layered strategy right-sized for a single team: **one producer‚Üîconsumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the PRODUCTION code path** ‚Äî explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat. Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are out of scope (overkill for a single team).

## Child stories

This epic decomposes into six stories (sized for one PR each):

- **A ‚Äî Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client** (`fe57-7712-1e3f-45f4`): the foundation ‚Äî fixtures captured from real Jira, served through the production path.
- **B ‚Äî Producer‚Üîconsumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test** (`8937-eed3-c881-4ea1`): the keystone; also owns the documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact.
- **C ‚Äî Verified fake: contract tests against BOTH real and fake client** (`59ef-ff3e-5aea-47b1`): keeps the fake honest.
- **D ‚Äî Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one** (`33d0-1e2c-e059-4181`).
- **E ‚Äî Sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary** (`2359-ac1a-7c76-4f56`): depends on D.
- **F ‚Äî Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode "expected-bug" guards** (`822a-2bad-fe48-43e0`).

## Sequencing (MVP-first thin vertical slice)

The epic ships incrementally, not as a big-bang:

1. **A ‚Üí B scoped FIRST to the known seam** ‚Äî capture the inbound-comment (`comment`) and `issuelinks` shapes (bugs 0ee6/3f04) and land the single round-trip contract test on exactly those, before broadening the schema to all fields. This thin vertical slice de-risks the whole contract approach against the canonical failure.
2. **C** once the fake exists (keep it honest).
3. **D then E** ‚Äî telemetry builds on the single consolidated applier.
4. **F** reuses A's fixtures for the hermetic round-trip and de-encodes the probe guards.

Each story is independently shippable behind its own PR.

## Rollout & rollback

- The **fail-loud sub-op invariant** (story E) lands **warn-first** (telemetry + `WARNING`) behind a flag/canary, and is promoted to hard-fail only after a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings; revert = drop back to warn (no other code change).
- The **applier collapse** (story D) verifies behavioral parity ‚Äî the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did ‚Äî **before** the retired path is deleted.

## Success Criteria

- Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing.
- Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path.
- A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green.
- Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected.
- New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape.
- [ ] A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema.
- [ ] A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema.
- [ ] One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher ‚Üí snapshot ‚Üí differs ‚Üí applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads.
- [ ] Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains).
- [ ] The retired outbound applier path is deleted.
- [ ] Outbound sub-op telemetry (`links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied`) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome.
- [ ] A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback.
- [ ] Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path.
- [ ] Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B).
- [ ] All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green.
